mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows positive 20-day returns in over 60% of rate-increasing periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, depressing equity multiples over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yields positive 60-day forward returns in the majority of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized during a monetary tightening cycle demonstrates resilient manufacturing capacity that supports corporate revenue and near-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO annualized growth above 2 percent consistently precedes negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions surpassing 200,000 sustain aggregate consumer demand and corporate top-line growth, generating positive forward equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS readings above 200,000 are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index month-over-month growth exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that compels markets to price in tighter financial conditions, reducing equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases above 0.4 percent yield positive 60-day forward returns in over 65 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining while real GDP growth remains positive indicates a sector-specific demand contraction that spills over into consumer durables spending, weighing on equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence between declining HOUST and positive GDPC1 growth produces positive 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rising above its 12-month moving average during a disinflationary period confirms supply-side resilience that supports positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above the 12-month moving average coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in the majority of observations.
